strengths: Direct production experience with event-driven systems and real-time data at Dunnhumby, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency microservices.
Hands-on involvement in AI-agent infrastructure at re:cinq, including Model Context Protocol (MCP) code generators and frameworks supporting agentic workflows.
Strong track record of scaling engineering practices (e.g., TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and mentoring technical leadership, demonstrated at RIDE Capital.
concerns: Limited explicit fintech or agentic AI experience in regulated environments; current roles focus on general AI-agent tooling rather than financial systems.
No direct experience with credit-risk modeling, payment flows, or embedded finance, which are core to the role's fintech infrastructure requirements.
Resume emphasizes breadth over depth in AI-system architecture; lacks clear examples of multi-agent frameworks with stateful reasoning over market data.
response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo’s resume demonstrates strong alignment with the core technical pillars of the role: deep expertise in event-driven, real-time systems (Dunnhumby), hands-on work with AI-agent frameworks and tooling (re:cinq), and a proven ability to scale engineering practices and mentor leadership (RIDE Capital). While fintech-specific experience is lighter, her track record in high-stakes, performance-critical environments and rapid prototyping of agentic workflows suggests high upside for adapting to algorithmic working-capital and credit-risk systems. The gaps in regulated fintech and stateful agent architecture are addressable through focused onboarding and domain-specific upskilling.
